step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with revising our earlier selection. The key here is understanding where each cell originates from within different germ layers. Since both intestinal epithelial cells and goblet cells come from the endoderm, and while hepatic cells can indeed trace back to the same layer under certain developmental contexts involving the liver bud formation, odontoblast differentiation strictly involves dental mesenchymal tissues originating near the endodermal-derived oral cavity linings. Although hair follicles typically indicate ectodermal derivation, given our focus specifically on these particular cell types among those provided, option B remains correct since no other listed cell has a definitive non-endodermal origin.

Final Answer: B Based on thorough review and confirmation of cellular lineage associations, the final selected option accurately reflects the unique nature of odontoblasts' endodermal origin compared to the others mentioned. Final
